summary: This is a brief personal post where Joel Spolsky asks his blog readers for recommendations on renting a luxury villa in Greece for a family vacation. He specifies requirements including sleeping capacity for 10, proximity to a beach, and a self-catered kitchen for kosher meals. The post is notable mainly as an example of how early bloggers used their platforms as personal bulletin boards, leveraging their audience for crowdsourced advice.
key_points: Joel uses his popular tech blog as a personal channel to crowdsource vacation recommendations
The post reveals personal details about Joel's family size and kosher dietary observance
Demonstrates the early-2000s blogging culture where personal and professional content freely mixed
money_quotes: We're looking to rent a luxury villa on an island in Greece, walking distance to a nice beach, for a week or two in July/August, for the family.
key_insight: This is a purely personal post with no technical or professional content, illustrating how early influential tech bloggers mixed personal requests with their professional writing.
